description: You are an automation engineer for a medical device manufacturer upgrading the end-of-line packaging HMI for a surgical stent cartoner in Red Lion Crimson 3.0. Your task is to configure the HMI to parse raw 32-character Unique Device Identifier (UDI) strings into distinct data fields. Create a base String tag named 'RawBarcode' and four formula-driven tags ('ParsedSKU', 'ParsedBatch', 'ParsedLineStr', and 'ParsedLineInt') to extract the SKU, Batch, and Line Number based on the specifications found in the CrimsonTasks folder on the Desktop (udi_barcode_format_spec.txt, plc_integration_requirements.txt, and crimson_string_functions_cheatsheet.txt). Per the integration requirements, do not extract the 'PLANT' or 'DATE' fields. Save the project as 'med_device_udi.c3' in C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\. Login credentials: Docker / GymAnything123!

metadata.occupation: Packaging and Filling Machine Operators and Tenders
metadata.standard: FDA UDI Rule (21 CFR 801.20) / GS1 General Specifications
